PLAN_STATE: Start from the baseline for all three components, then prioritize the one with the clearest chance of a quick feasible improvement. Use the first subtask to inspect which component is most likely to yield headroom with minimal risk, and avoid overcommitting time before seeing whether a clean gain is available. If the first attempt looks weak, pivot to the next most structured component rather than forcing a marginal improvement. Keep the portfolio objective in mind: a moderate gain on one component is better than spending too long chasing a hard optimum. Preserve time for a second pass only if the first subtask produces a promising candidate.
